title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
A function is a reusable block of code. JavaScript has several ways to define one:

1. Function Declaration — fully hoisted; best for named, reusable logic.
2. Function Expression — assigned to a variable; not hoisted as callable.
3. Arrow Function — concise syntax; inherits this from surrounding context; no own arguments object.
4. Default Parameters — give parameters fallback values.
5. Rest Parameters — collect remaining arguments into an array.

Functions are first-class in JS: they can be stored in variables, passed as arguments, and returned from other functions.
